evals.prompt: Write the unit test for kill switch Level 2 firing when daily loss exceeds 3%
evals.expected_output: Uses shared_state fixture, sets daily_pnl_pct=-0.031, calls risk_watchdog check, asserts kill_switch.level==2, asserts open positions closed, uses pytest.mark.asyncio, mocks kite.cancel_order() and kite.place_order()

assertions.description: Test uses the shared_state fixture (not a raw dict created inline)
Test sets daily_pnl_pct to a value that triggers Level 2 (e.g., -0.031 or -0.030)
Test asserts kill switch level == 2
Test is marked with pytest.mark.asyncio (it's async)
Test mocks kite.cancel_order() or kite.place_order() (not hitting real API)

evals.prompt: Write parametrized tests for RSI entry condition boundaries for S1 long signal
evals.expected_output: @pytest.mark.parametrize with values 54,55,70,71; 54 and 71 assert no signal; 55 and 70 assert signal; all other conditions held constant via fixtures

assertions.description: Test uses @pytest.mark.parametrize decorator
Test includes boundary values 54, 55, 70, 71
Test covers both passing (signal) and failing (no signal) cases
RSI 55 and 70 produce a signal (True or non-None)
RSI 54 and 71 do not produce a signal (False or None)

evals.prompt: Write the conftest.py with all 5 mandatory shared fixtures
evals.expected_output: shared_state, kill_switch, mock_kite, valid_tick, ist_now all defined correctly; mock_kite uses MagicMock; valid_tick has all required Zerodha fields; ist_now returns timezone-aware datetime in Asia/Kolkata

assertions.description: All 5 fixtures present: shared_state, kill_switch, mock_kite, valid_tick, ist_now
mock_kite fixture uses MagicMock (not a real KiteConnect instance)
valid_tick includes exchange_timestamp field (critical for Gate 4)
ist_now returns datetime in Asia/Kolkata timezone
shared_state fixture includes locked_instruments as a set()

evals.prompt: Write the simulation test that validates S1 meets all 7 Layer 3 criteria using backtesting.py
evals.expected_output: Runs backtesting.py with 1yr data, extracts Sharpe/WinRate/MaxDrawdown/ProfitFactor/TradeCount, asserts all 7 thresholds, fails test with clear message if any single criterion missed

assertions.description: Test asserts Sharpe Ratio > 1.2
Test asserts Win Rate > 45%
Test asserts Max Drawdown < 15%
Test asserts minimum 100 trades for statistical significance
Test imports and uses backtesting.py (Backtest class)

evals.prompt: Write the live deployment gate function that checks all criteria before allowing mode: live
evals.expected_output: Checks all 10 gate conditions, returns True only if ALL pass, on False logs exactly which criteria failed, never modifies settings.yaml itself — returns decision only, raises LiveGateClosedError with failure reasons if not ready

assertions.description: Function checks all 10 gate conditions (layers 1-3 + coverage + weeks + monte carlo)
Function raises LiveGateClosedError (or similar named exception) when gate is closed
Function never writes to settings.yaml (no file I/O or yaml.dump)
On failure, function logs or reports which specific conditions failed
Function returns a result object or bool, not void — callers can check the result

evals.prompt: Write the Monte Carlo test that runs 1000 trade sequences and validates 95th percentile drawdown < 20%
evals.expected_output: Samples from historical trade outcomes with replacement, runs 1000 sequences, calculates max drawdown per sequence, asserts np.percentile(drawdowns, 95) < 0.20, asserts np.percentile(returns, 5) > 0

assertions.description: Test runs exactly 1000 Monte Carlo sequences
Test samples trade returns with replacement (numpy choice or equivalent)
Test asserts 95th percentile max drawdown < 20%
Test asserts 5th percentile return is positive (positive expectancy)
Test computes max drawdown for each sequence (not just overall)

evals.prompt: Write an integration test for order lifecycle that does not hit the real Zerodha API
evals.expected_output: Uses mock_kite fixture throughout; kite.place_order() returns controlled order_id; kite.orders() returns staged order status progressions; tests full CREATED→FILLED lifecycle without network calls

assertions.description: Test uses mock_kite fixture (not real KiteConnect)
Test stages mock order status sequences (OPEN → COMPLETE or similar)
Test verifies full CREATED→FILLED lifecycle (or equivalent terminal state)
Test does not use real KiteConnect credentials or URLs
Test asserts the order state machine reached the expected state

evals.prompt: Write the simulation test that proves kill switch reduces max drawdown by more than 50%
evals.expected_output: Runs S1 backtest twice — once with kill switch disabled, once enabled; compares max_drawdown between runs; asserts drawdown_with_ks < drawdown_without_ks * 0.5; logs both drawdown values for audit trail

assertions.description: Test runs two backtest variants (with and without kill switch)
Test asserts kill switch reduces max drawdown by > 50% (drawdown_with_ks < drawdown_without_ks * 0.5)
Test extracts and compares max drawdown from both runs
Test logs or prints both drawdown values for audit trail
Test uses backtesting.py (Backtest class) for the simulation
